tagline: Email, SMS, and push marketing in one B2C CRM platform

pricingIntel.summary: Free up to 250 contacts. Paid plans scale with list size. expect $700-1,500/mo for 50k-100k contacts
details: Free: up to 250 profiles, 500 emails/mo, 150 SMS credits
Paid tiers scale with contact list size (not publicly listed per-tier)
Separate SMS credits purchased on top of email plan
Enterprise pricing for large lists / high volume
costDrivers: Contact list size. the primary pricing lever, jumps significantly at 50k, 100k, 250k thresholds
SMS volume. credits purchased separately, $0.01-0.05/message depending on country
Add-on products. Reviews, Customer Hub, CDP features are separate line items

benefits.label: Email channel revenue
benefits.description: Automated flows (welcome, abandoned cart, post-purchase, winback) and campaigns generate attributable revenue from your existing email list. Industry benchmarks show well-run email programs drive 25-40% of total ecommerce revenue.

revenuePerSubscriber.label: Monthly revenue per email subscriber
revenuePerSubscriber.source: industry benchmark ($2-5/subscriber/month for active Shopify brands, Klaviyo benchmarks report)

benefits.label: SMS channel revenue
benefits.description: SMS flows and campaigns reach customers in a higher-engagement channel. Brands adding SMS to their existing email program typically see incremental revenue that email alone doesn't capture.

benefits.label: Tech stack consolidation
benefits.description: Replacing multiple point solutions (separate ESP, SMS tool, CDP, reviews platform) with one platform reduces total martech spend. Klaviyo case studies cite 18-30% savings.

martechSpendPct.label: Martech spend as % of revenue
martechSpendPct.source: dtcmvp estimate (DTC brands typically spend 2-5% of revenue on martech tooling)

consolidationSavings.label: % savings from consolidating tools
consolidationSavings.source: Klaviyo case studies: Daily Harvest 18%, Dollar Shave Club 30%

sources: https://www.klaviyo.com/pricing. free tier details, paid plans scale with list size
Daily Harvest case study: '18% savings in tech costs'
Dollar Shave Club case study: '30% lower total cost of ownership'
Tatcha case study: '20% YoY growth in ecommerce revenue'
Princess Polly case study: '2.8X global revenue growth'

narrative.headline: Klaviyo could drive {revenueTotal}/yr in channel revenue and save {costSavingsTotal}/yr for {brandName}.

byDepartment.Retention / CRM: For {contactName} on the retention side: this is your bread and butter. Klaviyo's email and SMS flows are where owned-channel revenue lives. The {revenueTotal}/yr estimate reflects what a well-run program should generate from {brandName}'s list size. If you are already running flows, the gap between your current attribution and this benchmark is your optimization opportunity.
byDepartment.Marketing / Growth: For {contactName} and the marketing team: Klaviyo is the engine behind your owned channels. The {revenueTotal}/yr represents email + SMS attributed revenue at industry benchmarks. The {costSavingsTotal}/yr in tech consolidation comes from replacing point solutions with one platform.
byDepartment.Finance / Accounting: For {contactName} reviewing the budget: Klaviyo's value to {brandName} comes from two places: {revenueTotal}/yr in channel revenue (email + SMS attribution) and {costSavingsTotal}/yr from consolidating your martech stack. At {targetMultiple} target, the ceiling is {maxMonthly}/mo.
byDepartment.Ecommerce / DTC: For {contactName} on the ecom side: Klaviyo powers your owned channels. The email program alone should drive the majority of {revenueTotal}/yr. SMS adds incremental reach to customers who do not open email. Together, they should account for 25-40% of {brandName}'s total revenue.
byDepartment.Founder & CEO: For {contactName}: Klaviyo is {brandName}'s owned-channel revenue engine. {revenueTotal}/yr from email + SMS, plus {costSavingsTotal}/yr in martech consolidation. At your {targetMultiple} target, the ceiling is {maxMonthly}/mo.
byDepartment.Other: Klaviyo drives {revenueTotal}/yr in email and SMS revenue for {brandName}, plus {costSavingsTotal}/yr in tech stack consolidation savings.

byCategory.Apparel & Fashion: Apparel brands typically see the highest email revenue per subscriber because of seasonal collections, new arrivals, and style-based segmentation. {brandName}'s email program should be one of the strongest revenue channels in the business.
byCategory.Beauty & Cosmetics: Beauty brands benefit from lifecycle flows (replenishment reminders, routine builders, shade-match follow-ups) that drive repeat purchases. SMS performs especially well in beauty because product launches create urgency that email alone cannot capture.
byCategory.Food & Drink: Food and beverage brands see strong subscription and replenishment revenue from email flows. SMS is effective for flash sales and limited drops. {brandName}'s higher order frequency means each subscriber touch is more likely to convert.
byCategory.Health & Wellness: Health and wellness brands see high lifetime value from email-driven subscription flows. Replenishment cadences (supplements, vitamins) are a natural fit for automated flows that Klaviyo handles well.
byCategory.Other: These numbers use industry benchmarks for revenue per subscriber. {brandName}'s actual email and SMS performance depends on list engagement, flow sophistication, and send frequency.

notes: Klaviyo is foundational martech. nearly every DTC brand uses it or a direct competitor. The SWAG shows what your email + SMS channels should generate and what you could save by consolidating tools. Pricing is list-size-dependent and not publicly enumerated; expect it to scale with your contact database.
